title: Consciousness as coordination (Chalmers, Bach, Nagel)
summary: The hard problem becomes a coordination problem: physical processes and subjective experience are bound by an attention/awareness layer. What we call consciousness is the meta-coordination between them — which is exactly what current AI lacks.
source: Ch08 8.3 — Chalmers (psychophysical laws), Bach (attention schema), Nagel (dual-aspect monism)

nodes.label: Attention Schema
nodes.description: Model of the system's own processing

edges.description: Experience is one aspect of awareness
Bridging laws couple physics to experience
Bridging laws couple experience to physics

identifiability_note: Subjective experience (n2) and psychophysical laws (n5) are both latent and confound the function->awareness path; this is the hard problem stated as a DAG.
